title: OPF Narrative Template
description: Schema for narrative template files in the pptx.dev library. Each template describes a named story arc (e.g. 'problem-solution', 'scqa') as an ordered list of beats. Templates are referenced from OPF documents via meta.narrative.template.

id.description: Stable slug used by OPF documents to reference this template, e.g. 'problem-solution'. Lowercase kebab-case.

summary.description: One-sentence description of when and why to use this narrative.

audienceFit.description: Audiences this narrative works well for, e.g. ['executives', 'investors', 'customers'].

durationRange.description: Typical talk-length window this narrative suits.

tags.description: Free-form labels for filtering and search, e.g. ['business', 'pitch', 'internal'].

preview.description: Visual previews of the record, used by picker UIs and inline rendering. All sub-fields are optional; engines fall back gracefully when previews aren't available.

src.description: Main preview image (PNG/JPG). Used as the primary visual in picker UIs and previews of the record.

thumbnailSrc.description: Smaller thumbnail preview suited to dense grid views.

vectorSrc.description: SVG / vector preview for crisp scaling at any size.

beats.description: Ordered list of beats that make up the narrative arc.

Beat.description: A single narrative beat — a labeled segment of the story arc with a specific dramatic purpose. Mirrors the NarrativeBeat definition in opf.schema.json so library entries and inline OPF beats are interchangeable.

id.description: Stable slug used by Slide.beat to reference this beat. Lowercase kebab-case.

description.description: Curator-written prose that explains what this beat should accomplish. Used as a prompt for AI-driven generation of slides assigned to this beat.
examples: Quantify the pain customers feel today, with one striking stat the audience can repeat afterward.

instructions.description: Short author-facing instruction for the beat — typically one phrase. Complements 'description'; AI uses this as a directive while 'description' supplies the rationale.

slideCount.description: Optional explicit slide count for this beat. Defaults to 1 when omitted; values >1 are reserved for beats that intentionally span multiple slides. Prefer decomposing a heavy beat into multiple beats over setting a high slideCount.

slideType.description: Default content kind for the beat's slide. Mirrors Element.type and helps engines choose a sensible layout when only the beat is specified.

layoutHint.description: Suggested layout id for the beat's opening slide, e.g. 'section-divider', 'title-slide', 'text-left'. Resolves the same way as Slide.layout — against catalogs.layouts and the default catalog at https://www.pptx.gallery/layouts.

placeholders.description: Which slide placeholders the beat expects to be filled. Helps the engine and AI decide what content to generate.

options.description: Beat rendering options that shape how the resolved layout is populated.

thoughtCues.description: Optional speaker prompts or thinking cues attached to the beat. Surfaced in presenter notes or AI prompts.
examples: What pain is the audience feeling right now?
Why hasn't anyone solved this yet?

BeatPlaceholders.description: Boolean flags indicating which standard slide placeholders the beat expects to fill. Engines may use these as content-generation hints; missing flags are treated as 'unspecified', not 'false'. Mirrors NarrativeBeatPlaceholders in opf.schema.json.

tag.description: Whether to render a small tag / label above the title.

title.description: Whether the beat fills the slide title.

subtitle.description: Whether the beat fills the slide subtitle.

slideImage.description: Whether the beat carries a dedicated slide-level image (separate from any content image).

BeatOptions.description: Engine-facing options that fine-tune how a beat's resolved layout is populated. Mirrors NarrativeBeatOptions in opf.schema.json.

multiple.description: How many parallel content blocks the beat expects in its layout (e.g. '2x' for a two-column beat).

showLabel.description: Whether to render the beat's label/tag on the slide.

showDescription.description: Whether to render the beat's description as supporting copy on the slide.
